name: Foreign transaction fee
summary: A fee assessed by credit/debit card issuers on purchases processed outside the issuer's home currency or through a foreign merchant/bank, typically 1-3%.

evidence.claim: CFPB's Truth in Lending Act guidance (Regulation Z, 12 CFR 1026.6) requires credit-card issuers to disclose foreign-transaction fees at account-opening and on each statement.
NerdWallet 2024 foreign-transaction fee survey: median US credit card charges 3%; no-FTF cards include Capital One Venture, Chase Sapphire Preferred, Discover (0%).
Visa/Mastercard network cross-border assessment fees of 0.8-1.0% are charged to the issuer and typically passed through to cardholders as part of the foreign-transaction fee.

body.description: A percentage surcharge (typically 1-3%) added to any transaction processed through a non-US merchant or in a non-USD currency. The fee combines the Visa/Mastercard network cross-border assessment (~1%) with the issuer's markup (0-2%).

disclosureLegality.requirement: Regulation Z requires disclosure on account-opening Schumer box and on each statement line where the fee is charged (E1).
Interchange Fee Regulation caps cross-border interchange at 0.2% (debit) / 0.3% (credit) and requires itemized disclosure.

negotiability.script: The better path is to switch to a no-foreign-transaction-fee card before travel (Capital One Venture, Chase Sapphire, Discover, Schwab debit). Calling the issuer to waive an existing FTF succeeds <10% of the time.
